name: Semantic Scholar MCP Server
what_it_does: Semantic Scholar MCP server enabling AI agents to search and retrieve academic papers from Semantic Scholar — Allen Institute for AI's free academic search engine covering 200M+ papers across all fields. Enables searching by keyword, author, or paper ID, retrieving paper metadata, abstracts, citation counts, and open-access PDFs, integrating academic literature search into research agent workflows.
use_cases: Searching academic literature for research topics from literature review agents
Retrieving paper abstracts and metadata for citation analysis from research agents
Finding related papers and citation networks from knowledge discovery agents
Accessing open-access full-text papers for analysis from reading agents
Identifying top researchers and institutions in a field from research intelligence agents
Tracking citation counts and paper influence from bibliometrics agents
not_for: Accessing paywalled papers (Semantic Scholar only surfaces metadata/open access; use institutional library access)
Real-time preprint monitoring (use arXiv MCP for very recent preprints)
Patents and technical standards (Semantic Scholar focuses on peer-reviewed literature)
best_when: A researcher or agent needs to search scientific literature — Semantic Scholar's free API provides 200M+ papers with semantic search, citations, and open-access PDFs without the copyright concerns of Sci-Hub.
avoid_when: You need full-text access to paywalled papers — use your institution's library database for legitimate full-text access.

auth.notes: Semantic Scholar API: free without API key (limited rate); API key for higher limits. Register at semanticscholar.org/product/api for free API key. Set S2_API_KEY environment variable.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5,000 requests/5 minutes unauthenticated; 100 requests/second with API key
pricing.notes: Semantic Scholar API is completely free (Allen Institute for AI). High-volume partners can request higher limits. MCP server is free.

known_agent_gotchas: Without API key, rate limits are strict (5000 req/5min) — register for free key for agent use
Paper coverage varies by field — CS/AI papers very comprehensive; some humanities fields less so
Open access PDFs available for ~50% of papers — others have metadata only
Semantic Scholar IDs differ from DOIs — translate between ID systems as needed
Author disambiguation is imperfect — verify author identity for important citation queries
Search relevance is good but semantic search quality varies by field and query complexity
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS Semantic Scholar API. Optional API key. Academic metadata — not sensitive. Free public academic resource from Allen Institute for AI. Legal alternative to Sci-Hub for paper discovery.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. Optional API key. Academic metadata is public. No sensitive data. Free AI2 API.
